# Generated by ollama (starcoder2)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

apiVersion: template.cnrm.cloud.google.com/v1beta1
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: template-for-cmn-app-stack
spec:
  values: |
    # Specify the GCP project ID that you created in the above step.
    gcpProjectID: ${gcp_project_id}
    computeNetworkName: ${compute_network_name}

    computeFirewallRuleExternalAccess:
      name: ext-access-${gcpProjectID}
      network: ${computeNetworkName}
      allowTcpPorts: 2001,3389
      description: Allow external access to GCP services
      targetTags: 
        - rdp-ext-access-target
    computeFirewallRuleInternalAccess:
      name: ext-access-${gcpProjectID}
      network: ${computeNetworkName}
      allowTcpPorts: 22443,2055,6971,8384,3389
      description: Allow internal access to GCP services

    computeAddressExternalAccessLBIP:
      address: ext-access-${gcpProjectID}-lbip

Compute Engine Deployment:
    name: vm-for-cmnappstack-deployment
    providerRef:
      name: gce
    templateURL: https://github.com/josh-beams/google-cloud-ops-sandbox/raw/main/tfplan/compute-vm-template-cmnappstack-deploy-v0.5.yaml
